summary: Iteration 18 - Certificate Training Name Placeholder Feature Testing. Verified that {training_name} placeholder is properly populated in certificates. All 15 backend tests passed after re-seeding preset templates.

minor.issue: Preset templates in database had old placeholder text without {training_name}
minor.resolution: Re-seeded templates via POST /api/certificate-templates/seed-presets to update with new placeholder
minor.note: This was a data issue, not a code issue - templates were seeded before the feature was implemented

critical_code_review_comments: Code implementation is correct - {training_name} placeholder is properly added in certificates.py (lines 146, 320) and certificate_service.py (line 304)
All 8 preset templates in certificate_templates.py correctly use {training_name} placeholder
Multi-module certificates correctly join module names with ' & '
seed_data_creation: Added second training session (sess_test_002) for mod_social_engineering to test multi-module certificate scenario. Re-seeded 8 preset certificate templates with updated {training_name} placeholder.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Certificate training name feature is fully working. Test user (a2be3723-95b8-4f00-bf33-583d8b46463a) now has 2 completed training sessions: mod_phishing_email (85%) and mod_social_engineering (90%). Preset templates have been re-seeded with {training_name} placeholder.

training_name_placeholder.single_module_name: PASS - Certificate shows 'Phishing Email Detection' for single module
training_name_placeholder.multi_module_names: PASS - Certificate shows 'Phishing Email Detection & Social Engineering Defense' for multiple modules
training_name_placeholder.filename_contains_module: PASS - Certificate filename includes module name (e.g., Phishing_Email_Detection)

template_placeholders.default_template: PASS - Default template has {training_name} placeholder in completion_text element
template_placeholders.all_preset_templates: PASS - All 8 preset templates use {training_name} placeholder

error_handling.non_completed_module: PASS - Returns 400 for module not completed by user

features_confirmed_working: Single module certificate displays specific module name (e.g., 'Phishing Email Detection')
Multi-module certificate joins module names with ' & '
